routing_score_source.reason: RF routing scores were replay-scored from the current saved pipeline artifact.

comparison.name: Frequency of sensor data / data flow rate sweep
comparison.repo_before: Missing. No runnable sweep in current repo.
comparison.evidence: KafkaConsumerRF.scala had a fixed batch interval and no rate sweep automation.

comparison.name: Spark batch interval sweep
comparison.repo_before: Partial only. Batch interval was fixed in streaming code.
comparison.evidence: No comparative batch-interval harness existed before the suite.

comparison.name: Estimated delay times under different flow rates and batch intervals
comparison.repo_before: Missing. No end-to-end or scheduling-delay matrix automation.
comparison.evidence: Current repo had no runnable delay instrumentation around Kafka/Spark replay.

comparison.name: Spark parallelism / core-count sweep
comparison.repo_before: Missing. local[*] existed but no core sweep test.
comparison.evidence: KafkaConsumerRF.scala and KafkaAnomalyDetection.scala were fixed local runs.

comparison.name: Kafka partition-count sweep
comparison.metrics: storage_memory, jvm_on_heap, tasks, task_time, elapsed_time, avg_input, avg_process
comparison.repo_before: Missing. No partition-count benchmark existed in the current repo.
comparison.evidence: No runnable topic partition benchmark or comparison script was present.

paper_alignment.note: Data-flow sweep uses the same six flow-rate values and reports tasks/time/avg input/avg process columns.
Batch-interval sweep matches the paper grid. Table 10 task/batch/job outputs and Table 11 Spark JVM/storage peaks are now collected from a direct Spark benchmark job.
The same flow-rate x batch-interval matrix is evaluated. Avg input/avg process and a delay matrix are produced with current runnable replay semantics.
The core-count grid matches the paper and Spark JVM/storage peaks are collected from direct Spark runs at each core level.
The partition grid matches the paper and Spark storage/JVM/task metrics are collected from direct Spark runs.

threshold_recommendation.selection_policy: Keep the anomaly threshold at the deployed default when possible, then choose the lowest review load subject to <=0.10 absolute effective-recall loss and <=0.01 absolute FPR increase versus the baseline.
threshold_recommendation.note: This is an operational threshold recommendation, not a model retraining result. It is intended to reduce uncertain/review load while keeping the RF alerting behavior close to the deployed baseline.
